title: Richly Spiced Vietnamese Tomatoes Bun Rieu
description: A salty and gooey Vietnamese dish featuring diced tomatoes and pickle, perfect for a monsoon day. This recipe is egg-free and friendly to various dietary needs.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the diced tomatoes, pickle, vegetable broth, rice vinegar, soy sauce, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the cubed tofu and cook for an additional 5 minutes, until heated through.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the bun rieu hot, garnished with chopped scallions.
Offer additional soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil on the side, for added flavor.
This dish can be made ahead and refrigerated for up to 3 days, or frozen for up to 2 months.
